summary: POS High-Speed Billing Feature Testing - Fixed critical bug in Quick Checkout. All P0 features working: Search focus ('/' key), Quick Cash (F2) opens modal, Complete checkout flow works, Items display correctly in grid with prices and stock.

ui_bugs.issue: Shows '₹NaN' for prices in Quick Add (1-9) favorites section
ui_bugs.cause: Items don't have selling_price at item level, only on variants. The Quick Add uses item.selling_price || item.price which is undefined
ui_bugs.suggested_fix: Use variant selling_price if available, or fallback to first variant's price

features_tested.feature: POS Page Loads Without JS Errors
features_tested.details: Page loads without JavaScript errors (only geolocation warning which is expected)
Pressing '/' focuses the search input correctly. Verified data-testid='pos-search' receives focus
Items with variants display correctly - Coke ₹40 (100 stock), Pizza ₹299 (30 stock), Burger ₹120 (50 stock)
Clicking item cards adds them to cart, shows 'Added to cart' toast, cart updates with item details and total
Cart shows correct subtotal, GST calculations, and total amount
Pressing F2 when cart has items opens Quick Cash Checkout modal

features_tested.feature: Quick Checkout Modal Shows Correct Totals
features_tested.details: Modal displays Items count, subtotal, total, and quick amount buttons (exact amount, rounded to 100, 500, 1000)
Clicking quick amount button processes sale, shows receipt modal with invoice number, clears cart, updates Today's Sales
Escape closes modals and clears search field correctly

bugs_fixed.fix: Created new quickCompleteSale(cashAmount) function that takes payment amount directly without relying on React state updates
bugs_fixed.reason: The original code called a non-existent handleSale function. Initial fix using setPayments + setTimeout didn't work due to React state batching

action_items: OPTIONAL: Fix Quick Add section ₹NaN display - use variant selling_price instead of item.selling_price
critical_code_review_comments: POSPage.js: quickCompleteSale() function properly handles cash payment without state dependency
Quick Checkout modal now correctly processes sales with proper invoice generation
Favorites section (Quick Add 1-9) is MOCKED - uses first 9 items as favorites, not connected to actual top-selling items API
seed_data_creation: Created Test Store (STORE001), Burger/Pizza/Coke variants with inventory (50/30/100 units)
context_for_next_testing_agent: POS High-Speed Billing features are working. Quick Checkout (F2) flow processes sales correctly. Favorites section shows ₹NaN due to items not having selling_price at item level (low priority). Test store 'Test Store' has inventory for Burger, Pizza, Coke.

mocked_features.Favorites/Quick Add Section: Uses empty favoriteItems array initially, falls back to first 9 items as favorites. Not connected to actual top-selling items API (/api/sales/analytics/top-items)
